title: Protocol for the LC-MS/MS-based proteomics assay of samples generated using the MPLEx method
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ab6ph","text":"This protocol provides details on how the  LC-MS/MS proteomics Data Independent Acquisition (DIA) samples were run for the HubMAP program (HubMAP TMC Lung and Pancreas).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Proteins are extracted using a modified Folch extraction (Folch 1957; Nakayasu, et al., 2016) termed MPLEx. The protocol utilized is provided on the protocols.io titled: " Metabolites, Lipids, Protein extraction protocol (MPLEx) "
Extracted proteins are then processed using a urea/trypsin-based method: " Sample Preparation (Urea/trypsin digestion) for Proteomics - Mammalian Organisms "

5 µl is injected into the liquid chromatography electrospray ionization tandem mass spectrometry (LC-ESI-MS/MS) system, comprising a Thermo Vanquish Neo with a Thermo Astral mass spectrometer. Note: Vialed samples can be conserved for up to 3 months in a -20 ˚C freezer
The ESI source parameters are set as follows: spray voltage 2.2 kV in positive ionization mode; capillary temperature 300 °C; S lens RF level 40 arbitrary units.
The DIA mode parameters are set as follows: - Precursor isolation window 2 m/z ranges - MS range 380-980 m/z - AGC target : 5e5 - HCD collision energy 25% normalized
MS1 parameters are set as follow: - MS range 380-980 m/z - Resolution 240K - Automatic Gain Control (AGC) target : 5e6
The column used was a Thermo ES906 column held at a temperature of 45 °C. An injection volume of 5 uL was used.
All solvents and additives used were LC-MS grade. Mobile phase A (MPA) consisted of 100% water with 0.1% formic acid. Mobile phase B (MPB) consisted of 100% Acetonitrile with 0.1% formic acid.
The gradient used was the following %s %s

Calibration is done using Pierce‱ FlexMix‱ Calibration Solution. Mobile phase solvents are topped off between experiment sets (i.e., not during a sample set) to reduce drifting retention times. Blanks are run before the start of every new sample set, and QC (mammalian peptide lysate) is run systematically after every sample set.
The *.raw files generated are analyzed using DIA-NN. An example of protocol is provided here: " DIA data analysis in DIA-NN against a DDA library generated in FragPipe "
creator: Geremy Clair
